Experiment was performed at the HVEC
tandem accelerator of the former Ion Beam Facility at
Deuterium gas targets of 3.0 cm length with an
entrance foil of 5.3 mg/cm2 molybdenum and a
beam-stop made of gold 0.076 cm thick were used.
for neutron detection.The energy dependence of the
efficiency of the neutron detector was determined
pointwise by measuring neutron yields of the
Time of flight method was used to determine
neutron energy. The distance between the center of the
gas target and the center of the detector was 3.53 m,
the total time resolution was 2.1 ns
Correction for neutron interaction in air on the long
flight path and in the target structure was made using
Syst. uncertainty in monitor cross section
